<thought>
I‚Äôm at row 07, column D (marked ^). The goal (G) is at row 05, column F, but the direct path seems blocked: in front of me (north) is a lava cell at row 06, column D. To avoid lava, I should turn right to face east toward row 07, column E, which is floor (.) and not lava or wall.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see the goal at row 05 column B, but my current facing is upward (^) at row 07 column D. The cell directly in front (row 06 column D) is floor '.', so I can move forward safely.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I can see the goal G at row 06 column B, which is to my left from my current position (I‚Äôm at row 07 column D facing up). The cell directly in front of me (row 06 column D) is '.' so moving forward would approach the goal while avoiding the visible lava at row 06 column C. I‚Äôll move forward first.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
In the visible area, the goal `G` is directly to my left (row 07 column B), and the cell to my front is `.` (row 07 column D). Since I can‚Äôt see a safe path forward to the goal, the most direct move is to face left by turning counter-clockwise so I can move toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal (G) is at row 05 column D. From my position at row 07 column D (marked ^), it is straight up: cells ahead are row 06 column D = 'L' (lava), so I can‚Äôt move forward. I need to go around: the cell to my left (row 07 column C) is '.' and to my right (row 07 column E) is '#', so the only safe immediate move is left. I‚Äôm currently facing up, so to move left I should turn left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I can see the goal at row 07 column F. From my position (row 07 column D, facing up), the cell directly in front is row 06 column D (.) which is safe. The cell to the right (row 07 column E) is lava (L), so I should first move forward toward the safe corridor that leads to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I can see the goal is not in my current local view. I‚Äôm currently facing up (^) and the cell in front (row 06, column D) is floor '.', so moving forward is safe and likely to bring me closer to new areas. I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I see the agent `^` in row 07 column D. The goal `G` is not visible in this local map, and the cell immediately in front (row 06 column D) is a wall `#`, so moving forward is blocked. To make progress and search for a path, I should turn to change direction rather than attempt the blocked move.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I‚Äôm at row 07 column D (marked ^), facing north. The cell directly in front (row 06 col D) is floor '.', so it‚Äôs safe to move. The goal is to the east (visible at row 05 col F), so advancing north is a good first step to approach it.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I can‚Äôt see the goal yet, but I‚Äôm at row 7 under column D (the '^' is under D). The cell directly in front of me (to the north) at row 6 column D is '.' (safe), while moving into lava is not indicated in that direction. So I should advance forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I can see the goal `G` in the cell to my right (row 07, column F). The cell directly in front of me is a wall `#` (row 06, column D), so I cannot move forward. I should turn right to face the goal and move next.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is at row 05 column D, which is directly in front of me right now (my facing direction is north; I can see G one step ahead). The cell ahead is not lava or a wall, so I should move forward to reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>

</thought>
<action>

</action>

<thought>
I see the goal `G` at row 07 column C, which is directly to my left from my current position (my `^` is at row 07 column D). The cell immediately to the left (row 07 column C) is `G`, and it is not lava or a wall, so I should turn left and then move forward into the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I can see the goal (G) at row 06 column D, which is directly in front of my current position (row 07 column D). The cell in front of me is not lava (#/L), so I should move forward to reach the goal.
